name: context-rerank
prompt:
Add a deterministic, code-aware RERANK stage to the Context Compiler.

In agentrail/context/rerank.py expose:
- rerank_candidates(candidates, *, query, top_k) -> dict with keys: method (str), ranked (list), rejected (list), changed (bool)
  - each ranked item carries item['rerank']['signals'] with keys symbolOverlap, graphDistance, freshness
  - each rejected item carries item['rerank']['reason']
  - fully deterministic (no model call)

Intent: reorder retrieval candidates by code-aware signals so the most relevant survive a fixed token budget, lifting retrieval precision. changed is True exactly when the reranked order differs from the incoming retrieval order, and a candidate that defines a queried symbol outranks a higher-retrieved keyword-only candidate. The rerank is deterministic and carries no LLM model (its recorded model is None); its only signals are the deterministic code-aware ones (symbolOverlap, graphDistance, freshness). It keeps equal-or-fewer candidates than the raw top-K — it never pads — and records every rejected candidate with a reason. With the stage ON, precision_at_budget is strictly higher than with it OFF on the existing retrieval fixtures, at equal-or-lower budget and with no fixture regressing.

Wire it into agentrail/context/retrieval.py (query_context) and agentrail/context/packs.py (build_context_pack). The compiler contract must record pack['compiler']['rerank'] as a dict with these exact keys: status (the string 'reranked' when the stage ran), method (a new method name, distinct from the pre-rerank 'hybrid_lexical_rrf_authority_freshness'), rankedCandidateIds (the ranked candidate ids, camelCase), rejected (the rejected candidates, each carrying a reason), model (None), and signals. Rejected candidates also surface in query_context's excluded list. Gate the stage behind the AGENTRAIL_CONTEXT_RERANK environment toggle.
